title: esenbeckia pumila var. latifolia
intro: Esenbeckia pumila var. latifolia is a variety of the Esenbeckia pumila plant, a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America and is also known as the "yellow dogwood" due to its bright yellow flowers and small, woody branches. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and treat skin conditions. Esenbeckia pumila var. latifolia can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, allowing for the extraction of its medicinal compounds and providing various health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial effects.
